action_params.reason: reduce thermal load on m041 while fan 2 degraded; buys time for maintenance dispatch

data.reasoning_trace: m041 fan_anomaly warn/0.88 (rule_engine: fan_idx=2 @ 708 RPM vs 3000 threshold, peers 6400+). Environment agent: real_signal conf=0.72 — fan 2 sustained 503–1254 RPM over 30min, ambient stable 25.5–26.1°C, chip temp 89–93°C (oscillating but in tolerance), cluster peers m001/m003/m010 normal. Verdict: localized fan hardware fault, not site thermal. Specialist hinted alert_operator, but chip temps are already flirting with upper band on 3-of-4-fan cooling — a reversible 80%/60min throttle reduces thermal load, prevents drift into crit, and is cheap/undoable. Pairing throttle with operator alert for fan replacement scheduling. L3 within throttle floor (0.70) and single-miner scope.

m029 fan_anomaly warn/0.88 (rule_engine, fan_idx2 at 868 RPM vs 3000 threshold). Environment agent: real_signal conf=0.85 — fan 2 stuck (562–1204 RPM span) while peers run 6400+; site temp flat 25.3–25.9°C, zone miners m001–m012 show normal 5700–6300 RPM on all fans. Miner-local cooling failure, not ambient. One fan down means remaining fans carry extra thermal load; risk of chip temp drift if hashrate stays at 100%. Specialist hinted alert_operator, but I down-weight: throttle to 80%/60min is the least-intrusive effective mitigation — reduces heat production proactively, reversible, buys time for scheduled fan replacement. Also emitting operator alert for maintenance dispatch. L3 within bounds (floor 70%, well under cooldown).

action_params.recommendation: schedule hashboard replacement; m045 running at ~68% nominal on 2/3 (or 3/4) boards

data.reasoning_trace: m045 hashrate_degradation warn/0.85 (rule_engine: 70.45 vs 104.0 TH/s, ratio 0.68). Hashrate specialist: real_signal conf 0.93, severity crit — flat plateau across full 30min window (σ≈0.37), power proportionally reduced to ~2170W, temps stationary, fault_injected=hashboard_failure; textbook 1-of-3 board loss, not throttling/stall. Voltage specialist: noise conf 0.82 — 12V rail stationary (σ≈0.030V), no correlation to hashrate, rules out PSU/power-delivery cause. Synthesis: miner-local, board-level compute fault; both specialists converge on hashboard failure as root cause. Action: this is not a reversible degradation — throttling won't recover a dead board and the miner is already effectively self-throttled by the loss. Running degraded at 68% is not dangerous (thermals nominal, power reduced), so no urgent L3 intervention needed. Correct path is human-directed maintenance scheduling (board swap/RMA), which is an L4-class action. Emitting L2 alert with full diagnosis so operator can queue maintenance window. No autonomous action taken.

action_params.note: Stabilize remaining hashboards on m013 pending operator review of suspected hashboard failure.

data.reasoning_trace: m013 hashrate_degradation warn/0.85 — current 68 TH/s vs expected 104 (ratio 0.654, sustained 30+ min). Hashrate agent: real_signal conf 0.95, severity crit — unambiguous step-drop plateau, power scales proportionally (~2150W vs ~3100W expected), consistent with one of three hashboards offline; thermals flat, rules out thermal cause. Voltage agent: noise conf 0.82 — 12V rail stationary (σ≈0.037V), PSU clean; voltage is not the vector. Synthesis: issue is miner-local hardware (hashboard loss), not voltage/power/thermal. Not a pre-failure cascade — the failure already occurred and stabilized. Full shutdown not warranted (miner still producing 65% on remaining boards, reversible intervention preferred). Throttling to 70%/60min to reduce stress on the two surviving boards and cap thermal/electrical load while operator schedules hashboard service. Reversible, rate-limited, within L3 floor. Alerting operator in parallel is implicit — this is not a silent action. L3.

m033 hashrate_degradation warn/0.85 — current 70.6 TH/s vs 104 TH/s baseline (−31.6%). Hashrate agent: real_signal conf=0.93, sev=crit — flat plateau σ=0.41 TH/s, no thermal signature, magnitude matches ~1 hashboard loss on S19j Pro (104→~78), telemetry confirms hashboard_failure pattern. Voltage agent: noise conf=0.82 — rail stationary 11.94–12.08V, σ=0.031V, no correlation with power swings; rules out PSU/cap degradation as driver. Combined read: miner-local hardware loss (hashboard), not a degradation trend and not voltage-driven. Throttling a miner that's already producing 68% of nameplate achieves nothing — the capacity is already gone, this needs physical inspection/RMA. Not a safety emergency (temps nominal, no runaway), so shutdown is premature. Routing to operator with crit severity upgrade and maintenance recommendation. L2 — action is consequential (service ticket, likely board swap) and belongs to humans.

m010 fan_anomaly warn/0.88. Environment agent: real_signal conf=0.82 — fan 2 stuck at 700–1200 RPM vs 3000 threshold, sibling fans nominal ~6500 RPM, site ambient stable 25.3–25.9°C, chip temp oscillating 88–92°C with fault_injected=cooling_degradation. Miner-local mechanical cooling failure, not site HVAC. Haiku hinted alert_operator, but with chip temps already hitting 92°C on a confirmed failed fan, waiting on human approval risks thermal damage. Throttle 75%/60min reduces heat load on remaining 3 fans, reversible, within L3 floor (0.70). Concurrently this should be followed by scheduled fan replacement — surfacing to operator via the decision log, but acting now on the reversible thermal mitigation. L3.

m041 thermal_runaway crit/0.95, chip 92.15°C sustained 10min vs 85°C threshold. Environment (haiku) real_signal conf=0.78: miner-local cooling degradation, ambient 25°C stable, zone peers within normal band — not HVAC event. Hashrate (sonnet) noise conf=0.87: output stationary at ~104.7 TH/s, thermal governor has NOT engaged yet — meaning the chip is cooking without self-protection. Both specialists hinted alert_operator (warn), but I'm overriding upward: rule engine says crit at 92°C sustained, hashrate not throttling means no autonomous thermal relief, and the fault is confirmed miner-local (cooling degradation) so a local throttle directly addresses it. Throttling to 70%/30min — Flow 2 canonical response for ambient-normal thermal crit. Reversible, magnitude-capped, single miner. L3.

m029 thermal_runaway crit/0.95, chip 89.4°C sustained 10min (thr 85). Environment specialist: real_signal 0.82 — site ambient steady 24.6–25.1°C (baseline-normal), but zone-peer m010 also ~90°C with same fan anomaly pattern; localized cooling failure, not HVAC. Hashrate specialist: real_signal 0.82 — hashrate stationary at +3.3% vs nominal (σ~0.5, no degradation yet), but fan[2] stuck at ~500–630 RPM vs ~6400 on the other three fans, hot_time_frac=1.0, thermal_stress up to 0.42. Converged read: mechanical fan failure on m029, pre-throttle silicon, imminent DVFS/protection shutdown or device damage if untouched. Both specialists recommended alert_operator, but at 89°C sustained I will not wait on human ack — throttle to 70%/30min is reversible, magnitude-capped, within L3 floor, and buys thermal headroom while operator is paged to replace fan[2]. m010 shows same signature in same zone: flagging for operator review but not batching the action (stays under the 5-miner fleet-wide threshold and each needs its own fan diagnosis). L3 on m029, concurrent L2 alert for fan replacement + m010 inspection.

m010 thermal_runaway crit/0.95, chip 90.3°C sustained 10min (threshold 85°C). Environment (haiku): real_signal 0.82 — site ambient stable 24.3–25°C, zone peers m001–m008 at 66–82°C, no cluster movement; miner-local cooling degradation confirmed. Hashrate (sonnet): noise 0.88 — throughput stationary ~106 TH/s, +2% above nominal, no throttle signature or temp-hashrate coupling, explicitly defers thermal causality to env domain. Synthesis: isolated miner-local thermal fault, chip still producing but running hot with degraded cooling — classic Flow 2 path, ambient normal. Hashrate agent's "noise" verdict applies to hashrate curve only, not to the thermal reality (90°C sustained is real). Both hinted alert_operator, but per Flow 2 canonical pattern for crit thermal with ambient-normal, reversible throttle is the correct first move to shed thermal load before silicon damage. Throttle 70%/30min — floor of L3 envelope, short duration, reversible, gives operator window to inspect cooling path. L3.

action_params.notes: Likely hashboard failure on m045 (3-of-4 boards effective). Non-reversible repair required; throttle optional as stopgap to reduce stress on surviving boards pending operator decision.

data.reasoning_trace: m045 hashrate_degradation warn/0.85, ratio 0.682 (70.95 vs 104 TH/s). Hashrate agent: real_signal conf=0.93, severity crit — step-drop plateau over 30min (σ≈0.4 TH/s), power scales proportionally (~2180W vs ~3100W), temps/fans nominal; textbook hashboard loss (~3 of 4 boards). Voltage agent: noise conf=0.82 — 12V rail stationary, ~139mV p-p ripple tracks load inversely, PSU healthy, not causal. Combined read: miner-local hardware failure (hashboard), not power/thermal. Issue is not reversible by throttle — a board is gone, throttling won't restore capacity and autonomous throttle would mask a repair ticket. Escalating to operator for maintenance scheduling (board swap / RMA). L4 because action is physical service, not a knob I own. Surfacing voltage-agent's clean bill so operator can skip PSU diagnostics.

m013 hashrate_degradation warn/0.85 (rule_engine: 67.99 vs 104.0 TH/s expected, ratio 0.654 sustained 5min). Hashrate agent: real_signal conf=0.93, severity=crit — clean step drop to flat plateau ~67.6 TH/s across 30min, textbook hashboard-out signature (~−35%, consistent with 1 full board lost + partial on another, or 3-board model at 75%); temps stationary (chip 56-59°C), fans healthy, power scaled proportionally — hardware fault, not thermal. Voltage agent: noise conf=0.82 — rail stationary at 12.05V ±0.03V, z=-1.6σ on worst sample, no sag or ramp; confirms fault is board-level compute, not PSU. Synthesis: both agents agree on root cause (hashboard failure), zero disagreement on domain attribution. This is real hardware loss — a throttle won't restore the missing board and isn't the right lever; the miner is already running at ~65% of nameplate on its own. Maintenance ticket (board swap) is the correct action, which is L4-class. Escalating to operator with full trace; no autonomous action warranted. L2.

action_params.recommendation: Dispatch tech for hashboard inspection/replacement on m033; consider throttle-to-off of failed board via firmware if supported (L4). Interim: miner can continue running at reduced capacity or be throttled to reduce stress.
action_params.suspected_fault: hashboard_failure (1 board offline, possibly partial on a 2nd)

data.reasoning_trace: m033 hashrate_degradation warn/0.85. Hashrate agent: real_signal conf=0.97, severity=crit — flat step-drop to ~70.8 TH/s vs 104 nominal (−31.9%, z>>5), power proportionally reduced to ~2170W, temps benign and stationary; textbook hashboard failure (≥1 board offline). Voltage agent: noise conf=0.82 — 12V rail stationary oscillation ±2σ, no ramp/step, PSU healthy; explicitly confirms fault is downstream on the hashboard, not power delivery. Synthesis: strong agreement on the diagnosis — discrete hardware loss on a hashboard, miner-local, not power/thermal. This is not a degradation trend I can buy time on with a throttle; a board is already gone. Throttling won't recover hashrate and retiring/repairing is L4. Correct action is to surface to operator with the crit-candidate diagnosis and recommended dispatch. L2 suggest.

m041 thermal_runaway crit/0.95, chip temp 89.8°C sustained 10min (peak 91°C), threshold 85°C. Environment agent: real_signal conf=0.72, chip oscillates 87–91°C with ambient stable ~23.7°C (baseline 23.5°C) — not environmental; co-located m010 shows identical +2–3°C excursion, suggesting zone-level cooling degradation, miner-thermal stress confirmed. Hashrate agent: noise conf=0.88, trajectory stationary (104.6±0.4 TH/s, no sawtooth, zero co-oscillation with temp) — rules out thermal throttle feedback but explicitly defers thermal adjudication to environment. Synthesis: thermal concern is real (rule-engine crit + environment confirms), hashrate orthogonal and non-dispositive. Co-located m010 shows same pattern but cluster is only 2 miners — below fleet-wide L4 threshold. Per Flow 2: ambient normal → throttle 70%/30min L3 immediately to shed thermal load, reversible, buys time while operator investigates zone cooling. Also recommending operator follow-up on m010 cluster via alert channel.

m029 thermal_runaway crit/0.95 (chip 87.3°C sustained 10min, threshold 85°C). Environment specialist: real_signal 0.82 — miner-local, not site-wide; zone peers (m001/m003/m006/m010/m011) 11–16°C cooler on same 23.1–23.7°C ambient, ruling out HVAC. Hashrate specialist: real_signal 0.82 — curve stationary-elevated (~107.3 TH/s, +3.2% vs 104 nominal), no degradation yet, but chip temps 86.4–90.1°C over 30min with Fan 3 anomalously low (485–1219 RPM vs peer 6300–6550 RPM). Consistent story: degraded fan → cooling loss → pre-failure thermal ceiling, hashrate hasn't cracked yet. Both specialists hint alert_operator, but Flow 2 (thermal crit, ambient normal) calls for immediate reversible throttle to pull the ASIC back from the ceiling while operator is paged. Throttle 70%/30min — floor of L3 envelope, justified by crit severity + failed fan + sustained breach. Reversible, rate-limited, buys time for fan service ticket. Alert will follow; if temps don't drop within 30min, escalate L4 shutdown.

m010 thermal_runaway crit/0.95 (chip 88.6°C, 10min sustained >85°C). Environment/haiku: real_signal 0.92 — oscillation 87–90.5°C, ambient stable ~23°C, zone peers (m001/m003/m007/m011) flat at 73–76°C; miner-local, no cluster effect. Hashrate/sonnet: real_signal 0.82 — hashrate stationary ~106 TH/s (+2% above baseline, σ 0.46), fan channel 3 erratic 491–1246 RPM vs 6300+ on other three channels; textbook cooling_degradation, pre-throttle/pre-damage. Both specialists concur real_signal at crit, both hinted alert_operator, but chip is >3°C over threshold for 10min with confirmed fan failure — waiting on human ack risks damage. Throttling to 70% (L3 floor) for 30min is reversible, drops thermal load immediately, and buys time. Paired with operator alert for physical fan service (L4-class, queued separately). Single miner, reversible, within L3 envelope.

m045 hashrate_degradation warn/0.85. Hashrate agent: real_signal conf=0.93, severity crit — flat step-drop plateau to 70.6 TH/s vs 104 expected (−32.1%, σ 0.39), power proportionally reduced ~2180W, thermals/fans nominal; classic hashboard-offline signature (likely 1 large or 2 of 4 boards dead). Voltage agent: noise conf=0.82 — rail stationary 12.01–12.12V, no sag/ramp; PSU actually under-stressed because failed board sheds load. Both agents agree miner-local hardware fault, not voltage/thermal/power-supply causal. Synthesis: this is not a degradation trend, it's a completed hardware failure — throttling won't help (board is already gone), and hashrate cannot be recovered without physical service. Action is not a reversible tuning knob; it's a maintenance dispatch / retire-or-RMA decision. Flow 1 synthesis would suggest L3 throttle on a degrading trend, but the plateau shape means the fault has already landed — throttling a 3-of-4-board miner further destroys yield with no upside. Queuing for human: schedule hashboard inspection/replacement. L4.

action_params.recommended_followup: dispatch hashboard replacement; miner running at ~65% capacity on likely 3-of-4 boards
action_params.suggested_interim: consider controlled shutdown or leave running degraded pending maintenance window

data.reasoning_trace: m013 hashrate_degradation warn/0.85, ratio 0.649 (67.5 vs 104 TH/s). Hashrate agent: real_signal conf=0.95, severity=crit — textbook step-drop plateau (σ≈0.4), thermals stationary (chip 55-57°C, fans full), power scaled proportionally (~2.2kW vs ~3.3kW expected). Shape+magnitude+thermal-independence = hashboard failure (3-of-4 boards remaining). Voltage agent: noise conf=0.82 — 12V rail stationary (~12.05V, 162mV range), no sag/ramp, PSU healthy. Combined read: miner-local hardware failure, hashboard dead, not PSU/grid/thermal. Throttling doesn't help (already degraded, not a thermal/voltage stress case); shutdown+retire-board is the real fix but is L4 (hardware intervention). No cheap reversible action applies here. Alerting operator with crit-severity recommendation and full trace. L4.

m033 hashrate_degradation warn/0.85 (rule_engine, ratio 0.687 vs 0.8 threshold). Hashrate agent: real_signal conf=0.95, sev=crit — flat plateau ~71 TH/s vs 104 expected (−31.7%), σ=0.4 TH/s, textbook 1-of-3 hashboard loss on S19j Pro; thermals stationary, rules out throttle/pool. Voltage agent: noise conf=0.88 — 12V rail stationary (σ=0.027V), power draw normal; rules out PSU/upstream. Combined read: confirmed hardware hashboard failure, miner-local, non-thermal, non-electrical. Not reversible via throttle — throttling a miner already producing 68.7% of spec doesn't recover a dead board and just masks the fault. Correct path is physical hashboard replacement, which is a maintenance action requiring human scheduling. Severity effectively crit per hashrate specialist; no fleet-wide pattern. Alerting operator with replacement recommendation rather than autonomous throttle (no benefit) or L4 shutdown (miner still earning ~69% — operator decides economics of runtime vs swap). L4 because maintenance dispatch is consequential and not in my L3 envelope.
